title: synaedrys clementiana
side_effects: Synaedrys clementiana can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness and nausea due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can also lead to stomach pain, diarrhea, and vomiting. Additionally, its use can cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation and respiratory issues, in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils can also result in skin dryness and irritation, particularly on sensitive skin.
